comment_text.ud3.10:7.1: Thai editions read sabbupadhiṁ hi, which is supported by the Udānavarga.
comment_text.ud3.10:7.3: Accepting bhavā aparimuttā from the Buddha Jayanthi edition. For puthu, the commentary says bahū, visuṁ visuṁ vā.

root_text.ud3.10:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā uruvelāyaṁ viharati najjā nerañjarāya tīre bodhirukkhamūle paṭhamābhisambuddho.
root_text.ud3.10:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā sattāhaṁ ekapallaṅkena nisinno hoti vimuttisukhapaṭisaṁvedī.
root_text.ud3.10:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā tassa sattāhassa accayena tamhā samādhimhā vuṭṭhahitvā buddhacakkhunā lokaṁ volokesi.
root_text.ud3.10:2.2: Addasā kho bhagavā buddhacakkhunā volokento satte anekehi santāpehi santappamāne, anekehi ca pariḷāhehi pariḍayhamāne—
root_text.ud3.10:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.ud3.10:6.1: ‘Ye hi keci samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā bhavena bhavassa vippamokkhamāhaṁsu, sabbe te avippamuttā bhavasmā’ti vadāmi.
root_text.ud3.10:6.2: ‘Ye vā pana keci samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā vibhavena bhavassa nissaraṇamāhaṁsu, sabbe te anissaṭā bhavasmā’ti vadāmi.
root_text.ud3.10:7.1: Upadhiñhi paṭicca dukkhamidaṁ sambhoti, sabbupādānakkhayā natthi dukkhassa sambhavo.
root_text.ud3.10:7.3: puthū avijjāya paretā bhūtā bhūtaratā aparimuttā;
root_text.ud3.10:7.4: ye hi keci bhavā sabbadhi sabbatthatāya sabbe te bhavā aniccā dukkhā vipariṇāmadhammāti.

translation_text.ud3.10:1.2: At one time, when he was first awakened, the Buddha was staying in Uruvelā at the root of the tree of awakening on the bank of the Nerañjarā River.
translation_text.ud3.10:1.3: There the Buddha sat cross-legged for seven days without moving, experiencing the bliss of freedom.
translation_text.ud3.10:2.1: When seven days had passed, the Buddha emerged from that state of immersion and surveyed the world with the eye of a Buddha.
translation_text.ud3.10:2.2: He saw sentient beings tormented with many torments, and burning with many fevers
translation_text.ud3.10:2.3: born of greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.ud3.10:3.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud3.10:4.2: overcome by contact, speaks of disease as the self.
translation_text.ud3.10:4.3: For whatever it thinks it is,
translation_text.ud3.10:4.4: it turns out to be something else.
translation_text.ud3.10:5.1: The world is attached to continued existence, <j>overcome by continued existence,
translation_text.ud3.10:5.2: taking pleasure only in continued existence, <j>yet it becomes something else.
translation_text.ud3.10:5.3: What it enjoys, that is the fear;
translation_text.ud3.10:5.4: what it fears, that is the suffering.
translation_text.ud3.10:5.5: But this spiritual life is led
translation_text.ud3.10:5.6: in order to give up continued existence.
translation_text.ud3.10:6.1: Of the ascetics and brahmins who say that through continued existence one is freed from continued existence, none are themselves freed from continued existence, I say.
translation_text.ud3.10:6.2: Of the ascetics and brahmins who say that through nonexistence one escapes from existence, none have themselves escaped from existence, I say.
translation_text.ud3.10:7.1: For this suffering originates dependent on all attachment. With the ending of all grasping there is no origination of suffering.
translation_text.ud3.10:7.3: Mired in all sorts of ignorance, beings in love with being are not released from continued existence.
translation_text.ud3.10:7.4: Whatever states of continued existence there are—everywhere, all over—all are impermanent, suffering, and perishable.
translation_text.ud3.10:8.1: One who sees truly like this,
translation_text.ud3.10:8.3: gives up craving for continued existence,
translation_text.ud3.10:8.4: while not looking forward to nonexistence.
translation_text.ud3.10:8.5: Extinguishment comes <j>from the ending of all cravings;
translation_text.ud3.10:8.6: fading away and cessation with nothing left over.
translation_text.ud3.10:9.2: for that mendicant extinguished without grasping.
translation_text.ud3.10:9.3: Victorious in battle, such a one has defeated Māra;
translation_text.ud3.10:9.4: they’ve gone beyond all states of existence.”

variant_text.ud3.10:4.3: yena hi maññati → yena hi maññati (sya-all); yena yena hi maññanti (ne6:288 [Sāsanapaṭṭhāna]; pe2:15 [Sāsanapaṭṭhānadutiyabhūmi])
variant_text.ud3.10:7.1: Upadhiñhi → upadhiṁ (bj); upadhīhi (si, pts-vp-pli1); sabbupadhiṁ hi (sya-all)
